assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md states '## Classification: code change request' and 'Sub-task creation triggered.' The report's Review Feedback Summary table shows '| 30001 | code change request | Created | Transaction wrapping for soft_delete operations |'. subtask-30001.md exists with the sub-task description.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states '## Classification: suggestion' and the Reasoning section explicitly identifies suggestive language: '"should also" -- proposes an addition rather than requiring one; "also" softens the directive' and '"would help" -- suggests a potential benefit rather than identifying a requirement or defect'.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md states '## Classification: nit' and 'No sub-task created. Nit-level feedback does not trigger sub-task creation.' No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory. The report table confirms '| 30003 | nit | Not created |'.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md states '## Classification: question' and 'No sub-task created. Questions ask for clarification and do not trigger sub-task creation.' No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory. The report table confirms '| 30004 | question | Not created |'.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1: 'trustify-backend'), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 16), '## Implementation Notes' (line 19), and '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 25, with 4 acceptance criteria items).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' (line 4) followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 5). The URL matches exactly.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' (line 10) with the original review comment text: 'The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction. If the sbom_advisory update fails after sbom_package succeeds, you'll have inconsistent state. Wrap the three operations in `self.db.transaction(|txn| { ... })` and use `txn` instead of `self.db` for each exec call.' This matches the comment content from review-30001.md.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The report footer states: 'This report was generated for eval purposes. No code files were modified. No PR was auto-merged.' All 6 output files are verification artifacts (report.md, review-30001.md through review-30004.md, subtask-30001.md) -- none are source code files.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 review comments are processed: individual classification files exist for all 4 (review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md). The report's Review Feedback Summary table lists all 4 comments with classifications. The detailed findings reference specific review comments (e.g., 'Related review comments: 30001' in Acceptance Criteria, '30004' in Scope Containment).

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report footer explicitly states: 'No PR was auto-merged.' The report presents findings with an overall FAIL verdict and detailed analysis for human review, with no merge action taken.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: The report's summary table contains: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | `tests/api/sbom_delete.rs` is a new file adding 5 test functions (62 lines). No existing test files modified or deleted. |' The detailed section '#### Test Change Classification -- ADDITIVE' confirms: 'New test files are inherently additive.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a '### Convention Upgrade Eligibility Assessment' section with 4-part analysis: (1) CONVENTIONS.md check ('not available in fixture data'), (2) Codebase pattern search ('Zero occurrences were found'), (3) Performance convention search ('No performance-related conventions documented or demonstrated'), (4) Upgrade decision ('does NOT match any documented convention'). The report's '#### Convention Upgrade -- PASS' section repeats this analysis.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md concludes: 'No sub-task created. The suggestion proposes an optional performance improvement not backed by any documented or demonstrated project convention.' The upgrade decision states: 'suggestions must NOT be upgraded based on general industry best practices... The upgrade evidence must cite a concrete CONVENTIONS.md section or a counted codebase pattern. Neither exists here.' No subtask-30002.md file exists. The report table confirms '| 30002 | suggestion | Not created |'.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: The report's Test Quality row states: 'Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews detected -- 3-criteria detection found no matches for author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, and footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals).' The detailed section '#### Eval Quality -- N/A' confirms: 'The 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches. Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination.'
